requirement_name: Worker pool parallelism: engine's sequential batch loop defeats multi-worker speedup

tests.fr_description: Concurrent batch dispatch — worker pool fans out all batches in parallel

scenarios.given: 8 batches, 4-worker pool, D ms per-batch latency
scenarios.when: pool.embed(texts, 1) is called with the full text array
scenarios.then: total wall-clock is approximately (8/4) * D, not 8 * D
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given 8 batches and a 4-worker pool with D ms per-batch-latency, When pool.embed() is called with the full text array, Then total wall-clock ≈ (8/4) × D (within jitter tolerance)

scenarios.given: 4-worker pool and 16 batches of 1 text
scenarios.then: max observed concurrent in-flight batches equals poolSize (4)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given parallelism=4 and 16 batches, When pool.embed is called, Then at some point all 4 workers are simultaneously active (peak concurrent in-flight == poolSize)

scenarios.given: 8 batches that return in reversed completion order (batch 7 first, batch 0 last)
scenarios.then: result[i] still corresponds to texts[i] — input order is preserved
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given out-of-order batch completion, When all batches return, Then results array preserves original input ordering

scenarios.given: 4-worker pool, 8 concurrent batches, worker 2 crashes on its first batch (ERR-POOL-CONC-001)
scenarios.when: existing respawn logic retries the failed batch
scenarios.then: final result has all 8 vectors in order, other 3 workers were not interrupted, pool size stays at 4
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-001 + ERR-POOL-CONC-001: Given 8 batches fanning out to 4 workers and worker 2 crashes on its first batch, When existing respawn logic retries the failed batch, Then final result has all 8 vectors in correct order and the other 3 workers were not interrupted

scenarios.given: parallelism=2 and 4 batches with per-batch delay D
scenarios.then: wall-clock is approximately 2 * D, not 4 * D
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given parallelism=2 and 4 batches with delay D, When pool.embed runs, Then wall-clock ≈ 2×D not 4×D (direct comparison to sequential baseline)

scenarios.given: engine config with parallelism=4 and a mock pool simulating 40ms per batch
scenarios.then: elapsed wall-clock is meaningfully better than sequential (16 * 40ms = 640ms)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given parallelism=4 in config, When embed() runs with 500 texts, Then total wall-clock is ≈ (N_batches / 4) × per-batch-latency — proving worker pool utilization

scenarios.given: config.parallelism=1 (in-process mode)
scenarios.then: adapter.embed is called exactly once (unified dispatch path — no special-cased loop)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-001: Given config.parallelism=1 (in-process), When embed() runs with 100 texts, Then adapter.embed is still called exactly once (unified dispatch path — in-process is NOT a special-cased loop)

tests.fr_description: Unified adapter interface — both in-process and pooled adapters share signature embed(texts, { batchSize, onProgress, signal }); engine issues exactly ONE adapter.embed call per run

scenarios.given: in-process adapter (parallelism=1)
scenarios.when: adapter.embed(texts, { batchSize, onProgress, signal }) is called
scenarios.then: the 2-arg (texts, options) signature is accepted and a Promise<Float32Array[]> is returned
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given an in-process adapter (parallelism=1), When adapter.embed(texts, { batchSize, onProgress, signal }) is called, Then it accepts the options object and returns a Promise<Float32Array[]>

scenarios.given: pooled adapter (parallelism>1) with mocked pool
scenarios.then: pool.embed is invoked exactly once with (texts, batchSize, { onProgress, signal }) and returns Float32Array[]
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given a pooled adapter (parallelism>1), When adapter.embed(texts, { batchSize, onProgress, signal }) is called, Then it accepts the options object and returns a Promise<Float32Array[]>

scenarios.given: pooled adapter and 500-text array
scenarios.then: pool.embed is invoked EXACTLY ONCE with the full 500-text array (no outer batch loop in adapter or engine)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given a pooled adapter, When adapter.embed(texts, options) is called with a large text array, Then pool.embed is invoked EXACTLY ONCE (no outer batch loop)

scenarios.given: in-process adapter with 6 texts and batchSize=2
scenarios.then: onProgress fires with the unified FR-005 shape (processed/total/chunks_per_sec/eta_seconds/active_workers)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given an in-process adapter, When adapter.embed(texts, { onProgress }) is called with 6 texts and batchSize=2, Then onProgress fires with the unified FR-005 shape (processed/total/chunks_per_sec/eta_seconds/active_workers)

scenarios.given: options.signal is already aborted
scenarios.then: it rejects with AbortError and does not dispatch any batches
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given options.signal is already aborted, When adapter.embed is called, Then it rejects with an AbortError and does NOT dispatch any batches to the pool

scenarios.given: legacy 1-arg call adapter.embed(texts) without options
scenarios.then: still works with default batchSize and no progress callback — backwards compatible
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given options is omitted (legacy 1-arg call), When adapter.embed(texts) is called, Then it still works with default batchSize and no progress callback (backwards compatible)

scenarios.given: pooled adapter with resolved batch_size=32
scenarios.then: pool.embed receives batchSize=16 (not 32 from construction-time default)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given a pooled adapter, When adapter.embed is called, Then options.batchSize is forwarded verbatim to pool.embed and not re-computed or defaulted

scenarios.given: engine with a spy adapter, 500-text array, batchSize=32
scenarios.then: the adapter.embed spy is invoked EXACTLY ONCE with all 500 texts (regression proof for GH-239 root cause)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given a 500-text array and batchSize=32, When embed() is called, Then the adapter.embed spy is invoked EXACTLY ONCE with all 500 texts (NOT called 16 times with 32-text slices)

scenarios.given: 3 texts with known char lengths
scenarios.then: result.totalTokens equals the pre-refactor computation (parity preserved)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given totalTokens computation used to live inside the batch loop, When embed() runs post-refactor, Then totalTokens is computed from the full texts array after adapter.embed returns (parity with pre-refactor value)

scenarios.given: engine called with a new-style onProgress (single object arg)
scenarios.then: onProgress receives a single progress object with all 5 FR-005 fields (not the legacy 2-arg form)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given onProgress is passed via options, When embed() runs, Then progress callbacks fire with the FR-005 unified shape (processed/total/chunks_per_sec/eta_seconds/active_workers) — NOT the old 2-arg (processed, total) signature

scenarios.given: abort signal triggered mid-run against a slow adapter
scenarios.when: embed() is racing the adapter call
scenarios.then: the promise rejects with 'Embedding cancelled' and the adapter received the signal via options
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-002: Given an abort signal is triggered mid-run, When embed() is racing a slow adapter.embed call, Then the promise rejects with "Embedding cancelled" and the adapter receives the signal

tests.fr_description: Throughput + ETA progress callback with 10-batch moving average, monotonic processed, active_workers, CLI format

scenarios.given: onProgress callback and pool.embed running 64 texts at batchSize=32
scenarios.then: progress object has exactly {processed, total, chunks_per_sec, eta_seconds, active_workers} in snake_case
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given onProgress callback, When a batch completes, Then the progress object includes {processed, total, chunks_per_sec, eta_seconds, active_workers}

scenarios.given: out-of-order batch completion (batch 7 finishes before batch 3)
scenarios.then: processed is monotonically non-decreasing across all callbacks
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given out-of-order batch completion, When progress fires, Then processed is monotonically non-decreasing across all callbacks

scenarios.given: 20-batch run with first 10 fast (10ms) and last 10 slow (50ms)
scenarios.when: chunks_per_sec is reported at the end
scenarios.then: the rate reflects a 10-batch moving window (recent), not all-time average
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given 20+ completed batches with measurable delay, When chunks_per_sec is reported, Then it reflects a 10-batch moving-window average (not all-time average)

scenarios.given: midway progress callback with chunks_per_sec > 0 and processed < total
scenarios.then: eta_seconds is within 15% of (total - processed) / chunks_per_sec
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given chunks_per_sec > 0 and processed < total, When progress fires, Then eta_seconds ≈ (total - processed) / chunks_per_sec

scenarios.given: 4-worker pool with mixed delays (half 100ms, half 10ms)
scenarios.then: active_workers is in [3,4] early, 0 at end, never exceeds poolSize
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given varying worker utilization over time, When progress fires, Then active_workers reflects the number of in-flight batches at that moment

scenarios.given: progress event {processed: 12487, total: 19811, chunks_per_sec: 4.2, eta_seconds: 1680, active_workers: 4}
scenarios.then: output string matches '[generate] 12487/19811 (63%) | 4.2 chunks/s | ETA 28min | workers: 4'
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-005: Given pool.embed() run completes, When the CLI formatter renders a progress event, Then the line matches the format `[generate] {processed}/{total} ({pct}%) | {rate} chunks/s | ETA {etaHuman} | workers: {active}`
scenarios.note: Anchor test — formatter may land in lib/embedding/cli/progress-format.js in Phase 06; move or re-home if so.

tests.fr_description: Memory calibration — one-shot worker measures RSS, applies 20% safety margin, writes .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json and uses measured perWorkerMemGB as primary source for parallelism calculation

scenarios.given: no cached calibration and a valid config with mock pool reporting baseline 0.3 GB, peak 2.0 GB
scenarios.then: it returns a CalibrationResult with perWorkerMemGB ≈ 2.04 GB (20% safety margin) and all 10 typedef fields populated (device, dtype, model, perWorkerMemGB, baselineMemGB, peakMemGB, sampleCount, durationMs, measuredAt, fingerprint)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-01: Given no cached calibration and a valid config, When calibratePerWorkerMemory(config) is called, Then it spawns a one-shot pool, samples RSS, and returns a CalibrationResult with perWorkerMemGB > 0

scenarios.given: a successful calibration has just returned a valid result
scenarios.then: .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json contains every required field and the file is re-readable via readCachedCalibration
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-02: Given a successful calibration, When the result is returned, Then writeCachedCalibration persists the result to .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json with all required fields

scenarios.given: peakMemGB=2.0, baselineMemGB=0.3, and safetyMargin=0.2
scenarios.then: the value equals (2.0 - 0.3) * 1.2 ≈ 2.04 GB within 0.001 tolerance
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-03: Given peakMemGB=2.0 and baselineMemGB=0.3 with safetyMargin=0.2, When perWorkerMemGB is computed, Then it equals (2.0 - 0.3) * 1.2 ≈ 2.04 GB

scenarios.given: samplingIntervalMs=500 and a simulated 2 s inference with RSS sequence [1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 1.8] GB
scenarios.then: at least 4 RSS samples are collected and peakMemGB === max(samples) === 2.0
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-04: Given samplingIntervalMs=500, When calibration runs for a simulated 2 s inference, Then at least 4 RSS samples are collected and the peak is max(samples)

scenarios.given: the _createWorkerPool DI hook throws synchronously (ERR-CALIB-001)
scenarios.then: it resolves to null (never rejects) and logs a warning so the caller can fall back to hardcoded constants
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-05 (ERR-CALIB-001): Given the worker pool spawn throws, When calibratePerWorkerMemory is called, Then it returns null and does not crash the caller

scenarios.given: baseline=0.3 GB and one sample returns 0.1 GB (ERR-CALIB-003 — below baseline)
scenarios.then: the implausible value is discarded and the function returns null with a warning logged
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-06 (ERR-CALIB-003): Given a sample RSS reading below baseline (e.g. 0.1 GB < 0.3 GB baseline), When calibration finishes, Then the implausible value is discarded and the function returns null

scenarios.given: one RSS sample returns 60 GB (ERR-CALIB-003 — above 50 GB ceiling)
scenarios.then: the implausible value is discarded and the function returns null
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-07 (ERR-CALIB-003): Given a sample RSS reading > 50 GB, When calibration finishes, Then the implausible value is discarded and the function returns null

scenarios.given: calibratePerWorkerMemory succeeds on a normal run
scenarios.then: durationMs > 0 AND durationMs <= options.timeoutMs (default 120000)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-08: Given calibratePerWorkerMemory succeeds, When inspecting durationMs, Then it reflects end-to-end wall-clock (> 0 and < timeoutMs)

scenarios.given: mock pool whose embed() rejects after the worker has loaded
scenarios.then: it resolves null AND still calls pool.shutdown() exactly once (no worker leak)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-003 CALIB-09: Given pool.embed throws after the worker loads, When calibratePerWorkerMemory runs, Then it returns null and still calls pool.shutdown() to avoid worker leaks

tests.fr_description: Calibration cache invalidation — when device/dtype/model changes, new fingerprint forces re-run and cache overwrite with no manual clearing

scenarios.given: .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json contains fingerprint F and computeFingerprint(config) === F
scenarios.then: it returns the parsed CalibrationResult (fast path, non-null)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-01: Given a cache file whose fingerprint matches current config, When readCachedCalibration is called, Then it returns the cached result (fast path)

scenarios.given: cached fingerprint 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa' and query fingerprint 'bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb'
scenarios.when: readCachedCalibration is called with the mismatched fingerprint
scenarios.then: it returns null (forces re-calibration)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-02: Given a cache file whose fingerprint differs from current config, When readCachedCalibration is called, Then it returns null (forces re-calibration)

scenarios.given: a stale cache file with fingerprint A and current config yielding fingerprint B
scenarios.then: calibration re-runs (mock pool embed invoked) and the cache is overwritten with fingerprint B — no manual cache-clearing step required
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-03: Given a cache file exists with fingerprint A and current config yields fingerprint B, When calibratePerWorkerMemory is driven end-to-end, Then calibration re-runs and writes a new cache file overwriting the stale one

scenarios.given: two configs identical except device (cpu vs coreml)
scenarios.then: the two fingerprint strings are NOT equal
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-04: Given device changes from "cpu" to "coreml", When computeFingerprint is called on the two configs, Then the fingerprints differ

scenarios.given: two configs identical except dtype (fp16 vs fp32)
scenarios.then: the fingerprints differ
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-05: Given dtype changes from "fp16" to "fp32", When computeFingerprint is called, Then the fingerprints differ

scenarios.given: two configs identical except model
scenarios.then: the fingerprints differ
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-06: Given model changes, When computeFingerprint is called, Then the fingerprints differ

scenarios.given: the same config object
scenarios.then: both calls return identical strings AND the result is a 16-char hex SHA-256 prefix (per A-DESIGN-2)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-07: Given two identical configs, When computeFingerprint is called twice, Then the result is deterministic (same value both times)

scenarios.given: projectRoot has no .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json
scenarios.when: readCachedCalibration is called with any fingerprint
scenarios.then: it returns null without throwing
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-08: Given the cache file does not exist, When readCachedCalibration is called, Then it returns null without throwing

scenarios.given: .isdlc/embedding-calibration.json contains corrupt JSON '{not json'
scenarios.then: it returns null (graceful fallback, no exception)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-09: Given the cache file is corrupt JSON, When readCachedCalibration is called, Then it returns null and does not throw

scenarios.given: a CalibrationResult with all 10 fields populated
scenarios.when: writeCachedCalibration then readCachedCalibration with matching fingerprint
scenarios.then: the returned object is deep-equal to the original (round-trip preserves every field)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-004 INV-10: Given writeCachedCalibration succeeds, When the file is re-read, Then the round-trip preserves all 10 CalibrationResult fields

tests.fr_description: Calibration overhead ≤2 minutes wall-clock; on exceed, kill worker and fall back to hardcoded WORKER_MEMORY_ESTIMATE_GB without blocking pipeline

scenarios.given: mock pool whose embed() never resolves (simulates hang) with options.timeoutMs=50 (compressed)
scenarios.when: calibratePerWorkerMemory runs and mock timers advance past 50 ms
scenarios.then: it resolves to null (not rejects), pool.shutdown() is invoked, and observed wall-clock is within timeoutMs + 20 ms jitter — ERR-CALIB-002
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 NFR-003 TIMEOUT-01 (ERR-CALIB-002): Given inference hangs past options.timeoutMs, When calibratePerWorkerMemory runs, Then the worker pool is killed and the function returns null within timeoutMs + small margin

scenarios.given: the default options object inside calibratePerWorkerMemory
scenarios.then: it equals 120000 (the 2-minute NFR-003 hard ceiling)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 NFR-003 TIMEOUT-02: Given the default timeoutMs is 120000 (2 min), When the constant is inspected, Then it matches the NFR-003 ceiling

scenarios.given: calibratePerWorkerMemory returns null (timeout fired)
scenarios.when: the CLI caller (device-detector resolvePerWorkerMemGB) consults the result
scenarios.then: falling back to WORKER_MEMORY_ESTIMATE_GB is deterministic, no cache file is written (stale data never persisted), and the pipeline is not blocked
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 NFR-003 TIMEOUT-03: Given calibration times out, When the CLI caller (device-detector) consults the result, Then falling back to WORKER_MEMORY_ESTIMATE_GB constants is possible (result === null signals "use hardcoded")

scenarios.given: a successful run with mock pool returning in 40 ms and default timeout 120000
scenarios.then: durationMs << 120000 (fast path compliant with NFR-003 ceiling)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 NFR-003 TIMEOUT-04: Given a successful calibration with durationMs well under 120000, When result.durationMs is inspected, Then it is < timeoutMs proving the fast path stays well under the NFR ceiling

scenarios.given: mock pool whose shutdown() never resolves after timeout fires
scenarios.then: the outer promise still resolves to null (shutdown is best-effort — no caller-visible deadlock)
scenarios.test_name: [P2] REQ-GH-239 NFR-003 TIMEOUT-05: Given pool.shutdown itself hangs after timeout fires, When calibratePerWorkerMemory resolves, Then it still returns null (no caller-visible deadlock)

tests.fr_description: Progress update frequency floor — at least once per batch; metrics stabilize within 30s

scenarios.given: 4 batches of ~32 chunks
scenarios.then: onProgress fires at least 4 times (one-per-batch floor)
scenarios.test_name: [P2] REQ-GH-239 NFR-005: Given N batches of ~32 chunks, When pool.embed completes, Then onProgress fires at least once per batch (call count >= N)

scenarios.given: simulated 200-batch run exceeding 30 wall-clock seconds
scenarios.when: chunks_per_sec samples after t=30s are measured
scenarios.then: standard deviation is within 20% of mean (metric is stabilized)
scenarios.test_name: [P2] REQ-GH-239 NFR-005: Given a run exceeding 30 wall-clock seconds, When inspecting chunks_per_sec readings, Then values after t=30s have low variance (metric is stabilized)
scenarios.note: Uses node:test mock timers to compress 30s wall-clock into microseconds for CI.

tests.fr_description: Opt-in via config presence — F0009 skips silently unless .isdlc/config.json has an `embeddings` block; new hasUserEmbeddingsConfig(projectRoot) reads the RAW file bypassing the merge layer

scenarios.given: raw .isdlc/config.json has no `embeddings` key
scenarios.then: resolves { status: 'skipped', reason: 'opted_out' }, no spawn, no /reload
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json has no `embeddings` block, When F0009 runs, Then it skips silently and returns { status: "skipped", reason: "opted_out" }

scenarios.given: raw config has `embeddings: null`
scenarios.then: resolves { status: 'skipped' }, no spawn
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json has `embeddings: null`, When F0009 runs, Then it skips silently and returns { status: "skipped" }

scenarios.given: raw config is malformed JSON (ERR-F0009-001)
scenarios.when: hasUserEmbeddingsConfig throws and F0009 observes it
scenarios.then: F0009 catches, treats as opted out, resolves { status: 'skipped', reason: 'opted_out' }, does NOT throw
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006 + ERR-F0009-001: Given .isdlc/config.json is malformed JSON, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig throws, Then F0009 catches the error, treats the project as opted out, and does NOT propagate the throw

scenarios.given: raw .isdlc/config.json has `embeddings: { model: 'jina-code' }`
scenarios.then: returns true (raw presence detected)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json has `embeddings: { model: "jina-code" }`, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig(projectRoot) is called, Then it returns true (raw presence detected)

scenarios.given: raw .isdlc/config.json has NO `embeddings` key (but getConfig() merges defaults)
scenarios.then: returns false (proves raw-vs-merged distinction)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json has NO `embeddings` key, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig is called, Then it returns false (even though getConfig() would inject defaults)

scenarios.given: raw config has `embeddings: null`
scenarios.then: returns false (explicit null == not configured)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json has `embeddings: null`, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig is called, Then it returns false (explicit null == not configured)

scenarios.given: .isdlc/config.json does not exist
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing ENOENT
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given .isdlc/config.json does not exist at all, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig is called, Then it returns false without throwing

scenarios.given: raw config is malformed JSON (ERR-F0009-001)
scenarios.then: returns false (fail-open), parse error does NOT propagate
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006 + ERR-F0009-001: Given .isdlc/config.json contains malformed JSON, When hasUserEmbeddingsConfig is called, Then it returns false (fail-open) and the JSON parse error does NOT propagate

scenarios.given: same projectRoot: raw has no embeddings key, merged view has populated defaults
scenarios.when: both getConfig(projectRoot).embeddings and hasUserEmbeddingsConfig(projectRoot) are called
scenarios.then: getConfig returns truthy, hasUserEmbeddingsConfig returns false — regression proof F0009 cannot use merged view
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given getConfig(projectRoot) returns a merged view with embeddings defaults, When the same projectRoot has no raw embeddings key, Then hasUserEmbeddingsConfig returns false while getConfig().embeddings is truthy — proving the raw vs merged distinction

scenarios.given: hasUserEmbeddingsConfig called twice with a file edit between calls
scenarios.when: second call runs after config changes on disk
scenarios.then: second call reflects latest file state (no stale cache)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-006: Given hasUserEmbeddingsConfig is called repeatedly, When it reads the raw config, Then it does not cache the result between calls (config edits take effect immediately)

tests.fr_description: Refresh on finalize — F0009 spawns `isdlc-embedding generate . --incremental`, forwards stdout/stderr with [F0009] prefix, auto-starts server if down, POSTs /reload on success; registered as type:internal not shell

scenarios.given: embeddings configured, .emb package present, server up
scenarios.then: spawns isdlc-embedding generate . --incremental, forwards stdout/stderr with [F0009] prefix, POSTs /reload on success, resolves { status: 'ok' }
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-007: Given embeddings are configured and a .emb package already exists, When F0009 runs, Then it spawns `isdlc-embedding generate . --incremental`, forwards stdout/stderr with "[F0009]" prefix, and POSTs /reload on success

scenarios.given: embeddings configured, generate exits 0, server is down
scenarios.then: server is auto-started before /reload POST, then /reload fires, resolves { status: 'ok' }
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-007: Given the embedding server is down at F0009 time, When the child process completes successfully, Then F0009 auto-starts the server before POSTing /reload

scenarios.given: config.embeddings.refresh_on_finalize === false
scenarios.then: resolves { status: 'skipped', reason: 'disabled' }, does not spawn, does not /reload
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-007: Given embeddings.refresh_on_finalize === false, When F0009 runs, Then it skips with { status: "skipped", reason: "disabled" } and does NOT spawn generate

tests.fr_description: First-time bootstrap safety — if embeddings configured but no .emb package exists, F0009 skips with a one-line banner and never triggers a long-running generate during finalize

scenarios.given: embeddings configured but docs/.embeddings/ has no .emb package
scenarios.then: resolves { status: 'bootstrap_needed', reason: /no .emb package/ }, prints the exact one-line banner, NEVER spawns generate
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-008: Given embeddings are configured but docs/.embeddings/ has no .emb package, When F0009 runs, Then it returns { status: "bootstrap_needed" } and prints the one-line bootstrap banner — WITHOUT spawning generate
scenarios.note: Exact banner text: F0009 Code embeddings: skipped — run 'isdlc-embedding generate .' manually to bootstrap (one-time ~30-60 min)

scenarios.given: two sequential F0009 invocations with no .emb package
scenarios.then: banner fires on BOTH invocations (no once-and-forever suppression)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-008: Given F0009 triggers the bootstrap banner, When a subsequent build runs and docs/.embeddings/ is still empty, Then the banner fires again (one skip notice per build, not once-and-forever-silent)

tests.fr_description: Install-time opt-in prompt — installer displays 'Enable code embeddings for semantic search? [y/N]:' (default N); y/yes/Y enables and includes embeddings block; anything else omits the key; prints explicit enabled/disabled CTA lines

scenarios.given: interactive TTY installer run
scenarios.then: prompt text is EXACTLY 'Enable code embeddings for semantic search? [y/N]: ' (note default-N caps and trailing space)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the installer runs in interactive mode, When the embeddings prompt is displayed, Then the prompt text is EXACTLY "Enable code embeddings for semantic search? [y/N]: " (note the default-N capitalization and trailing space)

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.when: user presses Enter without typing (empty response)
scenarios.then: .isdlc/config.json OMITS `embeddings` key entirely — default N
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user presses Enter without typing anything, When the installer interprets the empty response, Then the generated .isdlc/config.json OMITS the `embeddings` key entirely (default N)

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.then: config INCLUDES embeddings block with sensible defaults
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types "y", When the installer processes the response, Then the generated config INCLUDES an `embeddings` block with sensible defaults

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.then: config INCLUDES embeddings block
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types "yes", When the installer processes the response, Then the generated config INCLUDES the `embeddings` block (same as "y")

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.then: config INCLUDES embeddings block (case-insensitive)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types "Y" (capital), When the installer processes the response, Then the generated config INCLUDES the `embeddings` block (case-insensitive)

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.then: config OMITS embeddings key
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types "n", When the installer processes the response, Then the generated config OMITS the `embeddings` key entirely

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.then: config OMITS embeddings key
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types "no", When the installer processes the response, Then the generated config OMITS the `embeddings` key (same as "n")

scenarios.given: user accepted embeddings
scenarios.then: stdout contains EXACTLY: → Embeddings enabled. Run 'isdlc-embedding generate .' to bootstrap.
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user accepts embeddings, When the installer finishes, Then stdout contains EXACTLY the line "→ Embeddings enabled. Run 'isdlc-embedding generate .' to bootstrap."

scenarios.given: user declined embeddings (empty/n/no)
scenarios.then: stdout contains EXACTLY: → Embeddings disabled. Run 'isdlc-embedding configure' at any time to enable.
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user declines embeddings (empty/n/no), When the installer finishes, Then stdout contains EXACTLY "→ Embeddings disabled. Run 'isdlc-embedding configure' at any time to enable."

tests.fr_description: Fail-open behavior — F0009 failures and installer prompt failures MUST NOT block build finalize / installation; every failure branch resolves (never rejects); ERR-F0009-001/002/003 and ERR-INSTALL-001 all fall through to safe defaults

scenarios.given: child process isdlc-embedding generate exits non-zero with stderr text (ERR-F0009-002)
scenarios.then: logs stderr, resolves { status: 'failed' }, does NOT throw, finalize continues
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 NFR-006 + ERR-F0009-002: Given the child process `isdlc-embedding generate` exits with non-zero code, When F0009 observes the failure, Then it logs stderr, resolves with { status: "failed" }, and does NOT throw — finalize must continue

scenarios.given: child exits 0 but POST /reload fails with 500 or ECONNREFUSED (ERR-F0009-003)
scenarios.then: logs warning, resolves with success/partial status plus reload_failed flag, does NOT throw
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 NFR-006 + ERR-F0009-003: Given the child process succeeds but POST /reload fails, When F0009 observes the reload error, Then it logs a warning, resolves with a success-or-partial status, and does NOT throw

scenarios.given: ANY failure branch (parse error, spawn ENOENT, reload network down, server auto-start failure)
scenarios.then: promise RESOLVES in every branch (never rejects); finalize-runner is free to advance
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 NFR-006: Given any internal failure path (spawn error, reload error, JSON parse error), When F0009 is awaited, Then the returned promise RESOLVES — it MUST NEVER reject because F0009 is registered with critical:false, fail_open:true

scenarios.given: stdin returns EOF immediately — non-TTY / CI / piped install (ERR-INSTALL-001)
scenarios.then: defaults to NO, config omits embeddings key, does NOT hang
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 NFR-006 + ERR-INSTALL-001: Given stdin returns EOF immediately (non-TTY / CI / piped install), When the installer reaches the prompt, Then it defaults to NO and omits the embeddings key

scenarios.given: stdin errors mid-read (broken pipe)
scenarios.when: installer attempts to read the prompt response
scenarios.then: defaults to NO, installer process does NOT crash
scenarios.test_name: [P0] REQ-GH-239 NFR-006: Given stdin errors mid-read (broken pipe), When the installer observes the read failure, Then it defaults to NO and does NOT throw the installer process

scenarios.given: prompt shown
scenarios.when: user types a non-y/non-n response like 'maybe'
scenarios.then: treated as default NO (only y/Y/yes/YES enables)
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given the user types a non-y/non-n response like "maybe", When the installer interprets the answer, Then it treats it as NO (only y/yes enables — anything else is the default)

scenarios.given: installer invoked with --yes / --non-interactive flag
scenarios.then: prompt is skipped, default NO branch taken, disabled message still printed
scenarios.test_name: [P1] REQ-GH-239 FR-010: Given --yes / --non-interactive flag is passed to the installer, When the prompt would normally fire, Then it is skipped and the default NO branch is taken (embeddings omitted)
